---
title: "HADDOCK analysis"
date: 2026-06-20
categories: [docking, documented workflow, evaluation]
---

::: {style="position: relative;"}

:::: {.pixel-bar-frame style="--bar-v:url('/images/border_pink_vertical.png'); --bar-h:url('/images/border_pink_horizontal.png');"}

## What I worked on today

   I have been waiting on HADDOCK to finish this run for
   what felt like *the* longest time.

   Today, I have successfully worked through TrkB's domain architechture:

   - LRR domain: acts as the structural scaffold. It is capped by disulfide bridges
   via cysteine clusters CC1 and CC2.
   - d5 domain: this is the actual contact site of BDNF with the TrkB receptor
   - transmembrane helix: remains inside the membrane
   - kinase domain: in a dimer, two kinase domains form an activation loop.
   this activation loop blocks the substrate site.
   
   Following that, 

 

::::

![](/images/gem_green.png){.sparkle-corner style="top: -15px; right: -15px; animation-delay: 0.6s"}
![](/images/heart_pink.png){.sparkle-corner style="bottom: 45px; right: 0px; animation-delay: 0.3"}
![](/images/gem_teal.png){.sparkle-corner style="top: 150px; left: -15px; animation-delay: 0.5s"}

:::

::::: {style="position: relative;"}
